content:
Alright, so this low here, right here, and here. Notice those are relative equal lows. Well, why didn't it go

section_title: BULLISH MARKET STRUCTURE AND FAIR VALUE GAP MECHANICS
content:
So we have a low that was taken out here. We don't need this low taken out. Why? Because it shifts above
this low, I'm sorry, this high. Market structure is now bullish. Does it leave a fair value Gap? Yes, it trades
down to halfway which is consequent encroachment of the gap. Some of this is going to go right over your
head, these terms. I promise you, I'm talking about all these things continuously and explaining what they
are. Three second of down closed candles is a bullish order block, which is also midpoint of that Gap. Gap
is defined by these candles low and this candle's high, the one single candle in the middle, that's the fair
value gap.

section_title: VOLUME IMBALANCE AND PROPULSION BLOCK EXPLAINED
content:
Market rallies, creates a small little volume imbalance in here. I like that. Here is this down closed candle
which is a propulsion block, okay? Propulsion block is a down closed candle that approaches or goes into a
previous order block that's lower, which is what we have here. I'd like to see nothing more than half of that
candle be traded to, and we see that occurring here. Look at the bodies. Yes, we get a wick, but look at the
bodies expecting return back to this midpoint short. But look at the precision there, that is perfect. That's
algorithmic.

section_title: OHLC AND TIME
content:
When you look at how I look at price this year, you're never going to look at a Candlestick chart ever again
the same way. You're not even going to reach for a bull flag, you're not going to look for a head and
shoulders pattern, you're never going to reach for a diagonal trend line. Those things are going to be cast
aside. And you want to focus in on the Open, High, Low, and Close. That's all the algorithm is working with,
and it's using time, okay? So the time of day here, we're approaching the equities opening. We have
relative equal highs here. What's above that? Buy side liquidity. And more specifically, those orange levels
are a five minute fair value Gap. We're bullish, we want to see it trade through that so it'll act as a speed
bump but it's not a barrier or a brick wall. So you can see it's happening here. It stops, it consolidates,
rallies up above it, and comes right back down into what this candle right here is a breaker.

section_title: BREAKER BLOCK AND LONG ACCUMULATION
content:
We have a low, high, lower low. So sell side below these lows here has been taken out, it's been absorbed,
and then we went higher. Find the breaker, which is the up close candle inside the swing High between the
low and the lower low. All of this is taught in my channel. Find the midpoint of that, that is mean threshold. It
trades down into it here, and I'm accumulating long positions here within this volume imbalance as well.

section_title: EXECUTIONS AND TARGET
content:
This is what you watch me do in the morning recording where I was accumulating my long positions here,
talking about how it can go down to consequent encroachment midpoint here, that that's permissible, and
then we would see it rally up into this shaded area here. There's my executions, and then later in the day, I

section_title: LIQUIDITY VOID VS. VOLUME IMBALANCE & MARKET APPROACH
content:
So the market does in fact vault higher. Look at the bodies respecting the fair value gap I mentioned ahead
of time this morning. Comes back down. This move here is a buy side imbalance, sell side inefficiency. That
is not a liquidity void. It gets called that, it's not. This right here is a liquidity void. Let me take that off for a
second. See, there's a gap between this candle's high and closing price being the same thing, and this
candle's low and its opening same thing. There's no price touching or overlapping between these two price
points. That is a liquidity void. A volume imbalance is my range between where one candle's body and
another candle's body doesn't touch, but there are wicks that overlap in between. So there's the difference
between a volume imbalance and real liquidity void. They're mine, and you're not going to find them in
books until I mentioned them. Obviously, buy side imbalance, sell side inefficiency here. So it's inefficient in
what? Sell side. So you're going to be waiting for it to come back down. How far can it come back down?
Midpoint, which is consequent encroachment. So buy side imbalance, sell side inefficiency, consequent
encroachment. Already, these guys out there that want crossovers and moving analysis are already
thinking, 'This is too complex.' It's not complex. It's just you don't know the language yet. All we're doing is
looking for areas where the market's going to pull back into an inefficiency or run stops and go up to a level
to fill in an inefficiency or run stops. That's it. And the only way you have to worry about anything is first
determining what's the bias. That's where you determine is it likely to go higher, is it likely to go lower. And
then you just look for two different parameters, one or the other; they both don't exist. And then you simply
read the tape, you watch it go forward.

section_title: ATTACKING SELL-SIDE LIQUIDITY AND FUND ORDERS
content:
So balanced price range here. And then it's going to attack that low right there. Why should it be attacking
that low? Because this is where the majority of the move this morning started, and traders on a fund level
are going to have their orders down here. What kind of orders? Sell stops. They're not in the habit of
running up and trailing their stop loss because they're trying to catch more sizable moves. Well, what fund
manager is going to be holding that? You'd be surprised how a lot of them don't know what the hell they're
doing. Just because they're running a fund doesn't mean they're profitable. Just because they have a fund
doesn't mean they know what to do. So but they have a lot of money behind it, and that's where the
market's going to go, where the money is, where are the orders, not the harmonic crab patterns, okay? Not
these Elliott wave counts. You don't need to count anything, just count your profits. That's all you got to
worry about.

content:
Show up every single day, show up every single day with a notepad, and I promise you I will deposit more
understanding in you than you'll get from any other books out there that talks about technical analysis. And
I promise I'm going to do it in such a way where it will be fun, it'll be engaging. You'll have drills, homework
that you'll want to do, not like it was when you were in school. And you'll feel better about what you've
